-- stencil_ir.lua -- semantic stencil shape IR for stencil lowering.
--
-- Semantic SSA remains the Lua semantics authority. This module defines
-- hole-parametric operation shape consumed by normalization, contracts,
-- stencil metadata validation, and native emission.

local M = {}

local KNOWN_OP = {
  LoadSlot = true, StoreSlot = true, StoreI64Slot = true,
  LoadConst = true, ConstI64 = true, ConstI64Hole = true, ConstNil = true, ConstBool = true,
  Move = true, GuardI64 = true, GuardTable = true, GuardShape = true,
  GuardMetatableAbsent = true, GuardCallTarget = true, GuardArrayHit = true, GuardBounds = true,
  UnboxI64 = true, BoxI64Scratch = true,
  AddI64 = true, SubI64 = true, MulI64 = true, I64BinOp = true, I64UnaryOp = true, CmpI64 = true,
  FieldLoad = true, FieldStore = true, ArrayLoad = true, ArrayStore = true, BarrierCheck = true,
  ExitResidual = true, ExitBoundary = true, ExitUnlowered = true,
}
M.KNOWN_OP = KNOWN_OP

local DATA_HOLE_ROLE = {
  unknown = true,
  slot = true,
  slot_store = true,
  imm = true,
  const = true,
  bool = true,
  shape_offset = true,
  shape_id = true,
  metatable_offset = true,
  field_offset = true,
  array_base_offset = true,
  call_target = true,
  barrier = true,
}
M.DATA_HOLE_ROLE = DATA_HOLE_ROLE

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function role_name(kind, arg)
  if kind == "slot" then return "R" .. tostring(arg or 0) end
  if kind == "slot_store" then return "slot_R" .. tostring(arg or 0) end
  return tostring(kind or "unknown")
end

local Stencil = {}
Stencil.__index = Stencil
M.Stencil = Stencil

function M.new(source_ops, config)
  return setmetatable({
    ops = {},
    values = {},
    value_order = {},
    holes = {},
    hole_by_key = {},
    slotmaps = {},
    exits = {},
    facts = {},
    source_ops = copy_array(source_ops or {}),
    config = config or {},
    next_value = 1,
    next_hole = 0,
  }, Stencil)
end

function Stencil:new_value(ty, source, residency, facts)
  local id = "sv" .. tostring(self.next_value)
  self.next_value = self.next_value + 1
  local v = { id = id, ty = ty or "Unknown", source = source, residency = residency, facts = copy_array(facts) }
  self.values[id] = v
  self.value_order[#self.value_order + 1] = id
  return id
end

function Stencil:add_slotmap(op_idx, logical_slot, field_kind)
  if logical_slot == nil or field_kind == nil then return end
  logical_slot = tonumber(logical_slot)
  if not logical_slot or logical_slot < 0 or logical_slot >= 256 then return end
  local k = tostring(op_idx or 0) .. ":" .. tostring(logical_slot) .. ":" .. tostring(field_kind)
  self._slotmap_seen = self._slotmap_seen or {}
  if self._slotmap_seen[k] then return end
  self._slotmap_seen[k] = true
  self.slotmaps[#self.slotmaps + 1] = {
    op_idx = tonumber(op_idx or 0) or 0,
    logical_slot = logical_slot,
    field_kind = tonumber(field_kind or 0) or 0,
  }
end

function Stencil:hole(t)
  t = t or {}
  assert(t.role_kind ~= "exit" and t.role_kind ~= "fail", "exit/fail are control endpoints, not data holes")
  local key = t.key or table.concat({t.role_kind or "unknown", tostring(t.role_arg or ""), tostring(t.op_idx or 0), t.ty or "Unknown", tostring(t.semantic and 1 or 0)}, ":")
  local existing = self.hole_by_key[key]
  if existing then return existing end
  local id = self.next_hole
  self.next_hole = self.next_hole + 1
  local h = {
    id = id,
    role_kind = t.role_kind or "unknown",
    role = t.role or role_name(t.role_kind, t.role_arg),
    role_arg = t.role_arg,
    op_idx = tonumber(t.op_idx or 0) or 0,
    ty = t.ty or "uintptr",
    patchable = t.patchable ~= false and (t.patchable or DATA_HOLE_ROLE[t.role_kind or "unknown"] or false),
    semantic = t.semantic and true or false,
    key = key,
  }
  self.holes[#self.holes + 1] = h
  self.hole_by_key[key] = h
  return h
end

function Stencil:add(op, t)
  assert(KNOWN_OP[op], "unknown Stencil IR op: " .. tostring(op))
  t = t or {}
  local n = {
    id = #self.ops + 1,
    op = op,
    inputs = copy_array(t.inputs),
    outputs = copy_array(t.outputs),
    args = t.args or {},
    effect = t.effect or "none",
    source = t.source,
    hole = t.hole,
    guard = t.guard,
    exit = t.exit,
    deps = copy_array(t.deps),
  }
  self.ops[#self.ops + 1] = n
  if n.exit then self.exits[#self.exits + 1] = { op = op, source = n.source or 0, exit = n.exit } end
  return n
end

local function defined_values(st)
  local def = {}
  for _, id in ipairs(st.value_order or {}) do def[id] = true end
  return def
end

function M.validate(st)
  local errors = {}
  if not st then return false, {"missing stencil"} end
  local def = defined_values(st)
  for i, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    if h.id ~= i - 1 then errors[#errors + 1] = "non-contiguous hole id at index " .. i end
    if h.role_kind == "exit" or h.role_kind == "fail" then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "control endpoint encoded as data hole: " .. tostring(h.role_kind)
    end
    if (h.role_kind == "slot" or h.role_kind == "slot_store") then
      if h.role_arg == nil or tonumber(h.role_arg) == nil or tonumber(h.role_arg) < 0 or tonumber(h.role_arg) >= 8 then
        errors[#errors + 1] = "slot hole outside 0..7 canonical fact ABI: " .. tostring(h.role_arg)
      end
    end
  end
  for _, op in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if not KNOWN_OP[op.op] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown op " .. tostring(op.op) end
    for _, v in ipairs(op.inputs or {}) do if not def[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown input " .. tostring(v) .. " at op " .. tostring(op.id) end end
    for _, v in ipairs(op.outputs or {}) do if not def[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown output " .. tostring(v) .. " at op " .. tostring(op.id) end end
  end
  local has_slotmap = {}
  for _, sm in ipairs(st.slotmaps or {}) do
    if sm.logical_slot == nil then errors[#errors + 1] = "slotmap without logical slot" end
    has_slotmap[tonumber(sm.logical_slot)] = true
  end
  for _, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    if (h.role_kind == "slot" or h.role_kind == "slot_store") and not has_slotmap[tonumber(h.role_arg)] then
      errors[#errors + 1] = "slot hole without matching slotmap: " .. tostring(h.role_arg)
    end
  end
  return #errors == 0, errors
end

return M

-- ssa_to_stencil.lua -- lower optimized semantic SSA into hole-parametric Stencil IR.

local SIR = require("src.stencil_ir")
local IR = require("src.ssa_ir")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local FIELD_KIND = { a = 1, b = 2, c = 3, dest = 4, aux = 5, cur = 0 }

local SLOT_FIELDS_BY_OP = {
  MOVE={"a","b"},
  LOADI={"a"}, LOADF={"a"}, LOADK={"a"}, LOADKX={"a"}, LOADFALSE={"a"}, LFALSESKIP={"a"}, LOADTRUE={"a"}, LOADNIL={"a"},
  GETUPVAL={"a"}, SETUPVAL={"a"},
  GETTABLE={"a","b","c"}, GETI={"a","b"}, GETFIELD={"a","b"}, GETTABUP={"a"}, SELF={"a","b"},
  SETTABLE={"a","b","c"}, SETI={"a","c"}, SETFIELD={"a","c"}, SETTABUP={"b","c"},
  NEWTABLE={"a"},
  ADD={"a","b","c"}, SUB={"a","b","c"}, MUL={"a","b","c"}, MOD={"a","b","c"}, POW={"a","b","c"}, DIV={"a","b","c"}, IDIV={"a","b","c"},
  BAND={"a","b","c"}, BOR={"a","b","c"}, BXOR={"a","b","c"}, SHL={"a","b","c"}, SHR={"a","b","c"},
  ADDI={"a","b"}, SHLI={"a","b"}, SHRI={"a","b"},
  ADDK={"a","b"}, SUBK={"a","b"}, MULK={"a","b"}, MODK={"a","b"}, POWK={"a","b"}, DIVK={"a","b"}, IDIVK={"a","b"},
  BANDK={"a","b"}, BORK={"a","b"}, BXORK={"a","b"},
  UNM={"a","b"}, BNOT={"a","b"}, NOT={"a","b"}, LEN={"a","b"}, CONCAT={"a","b","c"},
  EQ={"a","b","dest"}, LT={"a","b","dest"}, LE={"a","b","dest"}, EQK={"a"}, EQI={"a","dest"}, LTI={"a","dest"}, LEI={"a","dest"}, GTI={"a","dest"}, GEI={"a","dest"},
  TEST={"a","b"}, TESTSET={"a","b"},
  CALL={"a"}, TAILCALL={"a"}, RETURN={"a"}, RETURN1={"a"},
  SETLIST={"a"}, CLOSURE={"a"}, VARARG={"a"}, GETVARG={"a"},
  MMBIN={"a","b"}, MMBINI={"a"}, MMBINK={"a","b"}, CLOSE={"a"}, TBC={"a"}, ERRNNIL={"a"},
}
M.FIELD_KIND = FIELD_KIND

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function slot_number(slot)
  if type(slot) == "number" then return slot end
  if type(slot) == "string" then return tonumber(slot:match("^R(%d+)$") or slot:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

local function source_op_name(op)
  return type(op) == "table" and tostring(op.op or "") or tostring(op or "")
end

local function build_slot_classes(source_ops)
  local concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences = {}, {}, {}
  local function class_for(conc)
    conc = tonumber(conc)
    if not conc then return nil end
    if concrete_to_class[conc] == nil then
      local cls = #class_to_concrete
      concrete_to_class[conc] = cls
      class_to_concrete[#class_to_concrete + 1] = conc
    end
    return concrete_to_class[conc]
  end
  for pc, op in ipairs(source_ops or {}) do
    if type(op) == "table" then
      for _, field in ipairs(SLOT_FIELDS_BY_OP[source_op_name(op)] or {}) do
        local conc = tonumber(op[field])
        if conc then
          local cls = class_for(conc)
          occurrences[cls] = occurrences[cls] or {}
          occurrences[cls][#occurrences[cls] + 1] = {op_idx = pc - 1, field_kind = FIELD_KIND[field] or 0, concrete = conc}
        end
      end
    end
  end
  return concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences
end

local function const_role_for_source(source_ops, pc, args)
  local src = source_ops and source_ops[pc]
  local on = source_op_name(src)
  if on == "LOADI" or on == "LOADF" then return "imm", "imm" end
  if on == "ADDI" or on == "SHLI" or on == "SHRI" then return "imm", "sC" end
  if on:match("K$") or on == "EQK" then return "const", "k_i64" end
  if on == "JMP" then return "imm", "sJ" end
  return nil, nil
end

function M.lower(ssa_result_or_graph, source_ops, config)
  config = config or {}
  local g = ssa_result_or_graph.graph or ssa_result_or_graph
  source_ops = source_ops or ssa_result_or_graph.source_ops or {}
  local st = SIR.new(source_ops, config)
  local concrete_to_class, class_to_concrete, occurrences = build_slot_classes(source_ops)
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = concrete_to_class
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = class_to_concrete

  for cls, occs in pairs(occurrences) do
    for _, occ in ipairs(occs) do st:add_slotmap(occ.op_idx, cls, occ.field_kind) end
  end

  local function slot_class(slot, pc)
    local conc = slot_number(slot)
    local cls
    if conc then cls = concrete_to_class[conc] end
    if cls == nil then
      cls = #class_to_concrete
      class_to_concrete[#class_to_concrete + 1] = conc or cls
      if conc then concrete_to_class[conc] = cls end
    end
    if not occurrences[cls] then
      occurrences[cls] = {{op_idx = math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1), field_kind = FIELD_KIND.cur, concrete = conc or cls}}
      st:add_slotmap(occurrences[cls][1].op_idx, cls, FIELD_KIND.cur)
    end
    return cls
  end

  local function canonical_guard(guard)
    if not guard or not guard.fact then return guard end
    local f = guard.fact
    local conc = slot_number(f.subject and f.subject.id)
    if conc == nil then return guard end
    local cls = slot_class(conc, 1)
    local nf = {}
    for k, v in pairs(f) do nf[k] = v end
    nf.subject = Facts.slot("R" .. tostring(cls))
    local ng = {}
    for k, v in pairs(guard) do ng[k] = v end
    ng.fact = nf
    ng.key = Facts.guard_key(nf)
    return ng
  end

  local function hole_for_slot(kind, slot, pc)
    local cls = slot_class(slot, pc)
    local occ = (occurrences[cls] and occurrences[cls][1]) or {op_idx = math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1)}
    -- Prefer same-op occurrence when possible; runtime slot patching resolves by op_idx.
    for _, o in ipairs(occurrences[cls] or {}) do if o.op_idx == math.max(0, (pc or 1) - 1) then occ = o; break end end
    return st:hole({ role_kind = kind, role_arg = cls, op_idx = occ.op_idx, ty = "slot", key = kind .. ":" .. tostring(cls) .. ":" .. tostring(occ.op_idx) })
  end

  local vmap = {}
  local function map_value(vid)
    if not vid then return nil end
    if not vmap[vid] then
      local vv = g.values and g.values[vid]
      vmap[vid] = st:new_value(vv and vv.ty or "Unknown", vid, vv and vv.residency, vv and vv.facts)
    end
    return vmap[vid]
  end
  local function new_output(vid)
    local vv = g.values and g.values[vid]
    local out = st:new_value(vv and vv.ty or "Unknown", vid, vv and vv.residency, vv and vv.facts)
    vmap[vid] = out
    return out
  end

  local consumers = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do if not n.removed then
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consumers[v] = (consumers[v] or 0) + 1 end
  end end

  local skip = {}
  for idx, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
    if n.removed or skip[n.id] then goto continue end
    local pc = n.source or 1
    local op, args = n.op, n.args or {}
    local ins, outs = {}, {}
    for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do ins[#ins + 1] = map_value(v) end
    for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do outs[#outs + 1] = new_output(v) end

    if op == "FrameLoad" then
      local h = hole_for_slot("slot", args.slot or "cur", pc)
      st:add("LoadSlot", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_read" })

    elseif op == "FrameStore" then
      local h = hole_for_slot("slot_store", args.slot or "cur", pc)
      st:add("StoreSlot", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_write" })

    elseif op == "BoxI64" then
      local fused = nil
      for j = idx + 1, #g.nodes do
        local m = g.nodes[j]
        if not m.removed then
          if m.op == "FrameStore" and m.inputs and m.inputs[1] == (n.outputs and n.outputs[1]) then fused = m end
          break
        end
      end
      if fused then
        local h = hole_for_slot("slot_store", fused.args and fused.args.slot or "cur", fused.source or pc)
        local box_out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
        local keep_tvalue = (consumers[box_out] or 0) > 1
        local fused_outs = keep_tvalue and outs or {}
        st:add("StoreI64Slot", { inputs = ins, outputs = fused_outs, source = fused.source or pc, hole = h, args = { slot = h.role_arg }, effect = "frame_write" })
        skip[fused.id] = true
      else
        st:add("BoxI64Scratch", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
      end

    elseif op == "GuardTypeI64" then
      st:add("GuardI64", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardTable" then
      st:add("GuardTable", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardShape" then
      local off = st:hole({ role_kind = "shape_offset", role = "shape_offset", op_idx = 0, ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      local sid = st:hole({ role_kind = "shape_id", role = "shape_id", op_idx = 0, ty = "u32", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardShape", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { shape_offset = off.id, shape_id = sid.id } })
    elseif op == "GuardMetatableAbsent" then
      local off = st:hole({ role_kind = "metatable_offset", role = "metatable_offset", op_idx = 0, ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardMetatableAbsent", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { metatable_offset = off.id } })
    elseif op == "GuardArrayHit" then
      st:add("GuardArrayHit", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardBounds" then
      st:add("GuardBounds", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard" })
    elseif op == "GuardCallTarget" then
      local target = st:hole({ role_kind = "call_target", role = "call_target", op_idx = 0, ty = "ptr", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("GuardCallTarget", { inputs = ins, source = pc, guard = canonical_guard(n.guard), deps = n.deps, exit = n.exit, effect = "guard", args = { call_target = target.id } })

    elseif op == "ConstI64" then
      local rk, role = const_role_for_source(source_ops, pc, args)
      if rk then
        local h = st:hole({ role_kind = rk, role = role, op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "i64", semantic = false, key = role .. ":" .. tostring(math.max(0, pc - 1)) })
        st:add("ConstI64Hole", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { role = role } })
      else
        st:add("ConstI64", { outputs = outs, source = pc, args = { value = tonumber(args.value) or 0 } })
      end
    elseif op == "LoadConst" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "const", role = "k_idx", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "const", semantic = false, key = "k_idx:" .. tostring(math.max(0, pc - 1)) })
      st:add("LoadConst", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { const = args.const } })
    elseif op == "ConstNil" then st:add("ConstNil", { outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "ConstBool" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "bool", role = "bool_val", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "bool", semantic = false, key = "bool:" .. tostring(pc) })
      st:add("ConstBool", { outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { value = args.value and true or false } })
    elseif op == "Move" then st:add("Move", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "UnboxI64" then st:add("UnboxI64", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc })
    elseif op == "AddI64" or op == "SubI64" or op == "MulI64" or op == "I64BinOp" or op == "I64UnaryOp" or op == "CmpI64" then
      st:add(op, { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, args = args })
    elseif op == "FieldLoad" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "field_offset", role = "field_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("FieldLoad", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, args = { key = args.key }, effect = "heap_read" })
    elseif op == "FieldStore" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "field_offset", role = "field_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("FieldStore", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, args = { key = args.key }, effect = "heap_write" })
    elseif op == "ArrayLoad" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "array_base_offset", role = "array_base_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("ArrayLoad", { inputs = ins, outputs = outs, source = pc, hole = h, effect = "heap_read" })
    elseif op == "ArrayStore" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "array_base_offset", role = "array_base_offset", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "offset", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("ArrayStore", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, effect = "heap_write" })
    elseif op == "BarrierCheck" then
      local h = st:hole({ role_kind = "barrier", role = "barrier", op_idx = math.max(0, pc - 1), ty = "bool", patchable = false, semantic = true })
      st:add("BarrierCheck", { inputs = ins, source = pc, hole = h, exit = n.exit, effect = "gc_barrier" })
    elseif op == "GenericExit" or op == "Residual" then
      st:add("ExitResidual", { inputs = ins, source = pc, args = args, exit = n.exit, effect = "residual" })
    elseif op == "Jump" or op == "Return1" or op == "Return0" or op == "Call" or op == "KnownCall" or op == "TailCall" then
      st:add("ExitBoundary", { inputs = ins, source = pc, args = { op = op }, exit = n.exit, effect = n.effect or "return" })
    else
      st:add("ExitUnlowered", { inputs = ins, source = pc, args = { op = op }, exit = n.exit, effect = "residual" })
    end
    ::continue::
  end

  table.sort(st.slotmaps, function(a, b)
    if a.op_idx ~= b.op_idx then return a.op_idx < b.op_idx end
    if a.logical_slot ~= b.logical_slot then return a.logical_slot < b.logical_slot end
    return a.field_kind < b.field_kind
  end)
  return st
end

return M

-- ssa_contract.lua -- stencil fact-transfer contracts from Stencil IR.
--
-- Contracts are over the same 64-bit ABI as runtime facts, but slot facts are
-- encoded in canonical stencil slot-class space. Generated stencil selector
-- code remaps these masks to actual runtime slots using stencil.slotmaps.

local Sig = require("src.fact_signature")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function slot_number_from_subject(subj)
  local id = subj and subj.id
  if type(id) == "number" then return id end
  if type(id) == "string" then return tonumber(id:match("^R(%d+)$") or id:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

local function remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st)
  if type(f) ~= "table" then return f end
  local slot = slot_number_from_subject(f.subject)
  if slot == nil then return f end
  local cls = st and st.slot_class_by_concrete and st.slot_class_by_concrete[slot]
  if cls == nil then return f end
  local nf = {}
  for k, v in pairs(f) do nf[k] = v end
  nf.subject = Facts.slot("R" .. tostring(cls))
  return nf
end

local function remap_facts(facts, st)
  local out = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(facts or {}) do out[#out + 1] = remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st) end
  return out
end

local function node_kills_all_vm_facts(n)
  if n.effect == "call" or n.op == "ExitBoundary" then return true end
  if n.effect == "heap_write" or n.op == "FieldStore" or n.op == "ArrayStore" then return true end
  if n.effect == "gc_barrier" or n.op == "BarrierCheck" then return true end
  return false
end

local function op_is_hard_exit(n)
  return n.op == "ExitResidual" or n.op == "ExitBoundary" or n.op == "ExitUnlowered"
end

function M.from_result(ssa_result, facts)
  local st = assert(ssa_result and ssa_result.stencil, "ssa_contract requires ssa_result.stencil")
  local selector = Sig.encode(remap_facts(facts or ssa_result.facts or {}, st))
  local checked = Sig.empty()
  local produced = Sig.empty()
  local killed = Sig.empty()
  local deps = {}
  local exits = {}

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.guard and n.guard.fact then
      checked = Sig.bor(checked, Sig.encode({remap_fact_to_canonical(n.guard.fact, st)}))
    end
    for _, d in ipairs(n.deps or {}) do deps[#deps + 1] = d end
    if n.exit then exits[#exits + 1] = {pc = n.source or 0, reason = n.exit.reason or "exit"} end

    if n.op == "StoreSlot" or n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local slot = n.hole and n.hole.role_arg
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.slot_kill(slot))
      if n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then produced = Sig.bor(produced, Sig.i64_slot(slot)) end
    end

    if node_kills_all_vm_facts(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end

    if op_is_hard_exit(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_slot_facts())
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end
  end

  local required = Sig.minus(selector, checked)
  return {
    selector_sig = Sig.with_literal(selector),
    required_sig = Sig.with_literal(required),
    checked_sig = Sig.with_literal(checked),
    produced_sig = Sig.with_literal(produced),
    killed_sig = Sig.with_literal(killed),
    deps = copy_array(deps),
    exits = exits,
  }
end

return M

-- fact_signature.lua -- canonical 64-bit selector/contract fact encoding.
--
-- This is the single source for the compact runtime fact universe exported by
-- SponBank.  Facts in the rich foundry lattice are projected into this fixed
-- 64-bit ABI.  A TileTemplate may also carry transfer masks over the same
-- universe: required/checked/produced/killed.

local bit = require("bit")

local M = {}

M.SLOT_FACT_BASE = {
  i64 = 0, is_i64 = 0,
  table = 8, is_table = 8,
  shape_known = 16,
  metatable_absent = 24,
  array_hit = 32,
  bounds_ok = 40,
  known_call_target = 48,
  key_i64 = 0,
}

M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT = {
  barrier_clean = 56,
  const_i64 = 57,
  key_const = 58,
  nonzero_i64 = 59,
  shape_payload = 60,
  array_payload = 61,
  call_target_payload = 62,
}

local SLOT_FACT_BASES = {0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48}

function M.slot_fact_bases()
  local out = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do out[i] = b end
  return out
end

function M.global_payload_mask()
  return M.from_bits({56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61, 62})
end

local function u32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  if x < 0 then x = x + 4294967296 end
  return x
end
M.u32 = u32

local function hex32(n)
  n = u32(n or 0)
  local hi = math.floor(n / 65536) % 65536
  local lo = n % 65536
  return string.format("%04x%04x", hi, lo)
end

function M.literal(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return "0x" .. hex32(sig.hi or 0) .. hex32(sig.lo or 0) .. "ULL"
end

function M.empty()
  return {lo = 0, hi = 0, pop = 0}
end

local function add_bit_mut(sig, bitpos)
  if not bitpos or bitpos < 0 or bitpos > 63 then return end
  sig._seen = sig._seen or {}
  if sig._seen[bitpos] then return end
  sig._seen[bitpos] = true
  sig.pop = (sig.pop or 0) + 1
  if bitpos < 32 then
    sig.lo = u32(bit.bor(sig.lo or 0, bit.lshift(1, bitpos)))
  else
    sig.hi = u32(bit.bor(sig.hi or 0, bit.lshift(1, bitpos - 32)))
  end
end

function M.from_bits(bits)
  local sig = M.empty()
  for _, bitpos in ipairs(bits or {}) do add_bit_mut(sig, bitpos) end
  sig._seen = nil
  return sig
end

function M.normalize(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return {lo = u32(sig.lo or 0), hi = u32(sig.hi or 0), pop = sig.pop or M.popcount(sig)}
end

function M.popcount(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  local n, x = 0, u32(sig.lo or 0)
  while x ~= 0 do x = bit.band(x, x - 1); n = n + 1 end
  x = u32(sig.hi or 0)
  while x ~= 0 do x = bit.band(x, x - 1); n = n + 1 end
  return n
end

function M.bor(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.bor(a.lo, b.lo)), hi = u32(bit.bor(a.hi, b.hi))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.band(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.band(a.lo, b.lo)), hi = u32(bit.band(a.hi, b.hi))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.minus(a, b)
  a, b = M.normalize(a), M.normalize(b)
  local out = {lo = u32(bit.band(a.lo, bit.bnot(b.lo))), hi = u32(bit.band(a.hi, bit.bnot(b.hi)))}
  out.pop = M.popcount(out)
  return out
end

function M.is_empty(sig)
  sig = sig or M.empty()
  return u32(sig.lo or 0) == 0 and u32(sig.hi or 0) == 0
end

function M.parse_slot(subject)
  local id = subject and subject.id
  if type(id) == "number" then return id end
  if type(id) == "string" then return tonumber(id:match("^R(%d+)$") or id:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

function M.fact_bit(f)
  local pred = tostring(f and f.predicate or "")
  local subject = (f and f.subject) or {}
  local slot = M.parse_slot(subject)
  local base = M.SLOT_FACT_BASE[pred]
  if base and slot and slot >= 0 and slot < 8 then return base + slot end
  if pred == "barrier_clean" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.barrier_clean end
  if pred == "const_i64" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.const_i64 end
  if pred == "key_const" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.key_const end
  if pred == "nonzero_i64" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.nonzero_i64 end
  if pred == "shape_eq" or pred == "field_offset" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.shape_payload end
  if pred == "array_base_offset" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.array_payload end
  if pred == "target_eq" then return M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT.call_target_payload end
  return nil
end

function M.encode(facts)
  local bits = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(facts or {}) do
    local bitpos = M.fact_bit(f)
    if bitpos then bits[#bits + 1] = bitpos end
  end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.with_literal(sig)
  sig = M.normalize(sig)
  sig.literal = M.literal(sig)
  return sig
end

function M.slot_kill(slot)
  slot = tonumber(slot)
  if not slot or slot < 0 or slot >= 8 then return M.empty() end
  local bits = {}
  for _, base in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do bits[#bits + 1] = base + slot end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.all_slot_facts()
  local out = M.empty()
  for slot = 0, 7 do out = M.bor(out, M.slot_kill(slot)) end
  return out
end

function M.all_table_and_payload_facts()
  local bits = {}
  for _, base in ipairs({8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48}) do
    for slot = 0, 7 do bits[#bits + 1] = base + slot end
  end
  for _, bitpos in ipairs({56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61, 62}) do bits[#bits + 1] = bitpos end
  return M.from_bits(bits)
end

function M.i64_slot(slot)
  slot = tonumber(slot)
  if not slot or slot < 0 or slot >= 8 then return M.empty() end
  return M.from_bits({M.SLOT_FACT_BASE.is_i64 + slot})
end

function M.remap_slots(sig, slot_map)
  sig = M.normalize(sig)
  slot_map = slot_map or {}
  local out = M.empty()
  for _, base in ipairs(SLOT_FACT_BASES) do
    for slot = 0, 7 do
      local bitpos = base + slot
      local word = bitpos < 32 and sig.lo or sig.hi
      local mask = bit.lshift(1, bitpos % 32)
      if bit.band(word, mask) ~= 0 then
        local dst = slot_map[slot]
        if dst == nil then dst = slot end
        dst = tonumber(dst)
        if dst and dst >= 0 and dst < 8 then add_bit_mut(out, base + dst) end
      end
    end
  end
  for _, bitpos in pairs(M.GLOBAL_FACT_BIT) do
    local word = bitpos < 32 and sig.lo or sig.hi
    local mask = bit.lshift(1, bitpos % 32)
    if bit.band(word, mask) ~= 0 then add_bit_mut(out, bitpos) end
  end
  out._seen = nil
  return M.normalize(out)
end

return M

content.text:
# Lua Interpreter VM Experiment

This directory contains the Lalin-hosted Lua interpreter VM experiment and a separate SpongeJIT foundry prototype.

Important status note: **the interpreter in `src/` is not currently wired to a JIT.** The VM is an interpreter/compiler experiment built out of Lalin regions. SpongeJIT lives under `spongejit/` as an offline native-stencil foundry prototype; it is not the execution engine for `src/vm_loop.lua` today.

## What is here

```text
experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/
├── src/                         Lalin Lua VM/interpreter implementation
│   ├── init.lua                 module loader
│   ├── constants.lua            tags, opcodes, error/status constants
│   ├── products.lua             runtime data layouts
│   ├── opcodes.lua              Lua 5.5-style bytecode dispatch regions
│   ├── op_handlers.lua          opcode handler descriptions/templates
│   ├── vm_loop.lua              interpreter loop and entry points
│   ├── regions_*.lua            value/table/call/parser/gc/etc. regions
│   └── api.lua                  VM API-facing regions
│
├── tests/                       standalone Lua/Lalin tests for the VM and prototypes
├── benchmarks/                  interpreter and prototype benchmark scripts
│
├── spongejit/                   separate SpongeJIT native-stencil foundry prototype
│   ├── src/                     offline SSA → stencil normal-form metadata pipeline
│   ├── puc/                     status note for removed PUC integration
│   └── Makefile                 SpongeJIT native-stencil test entry point
│
├── tools/                       older/shadow analysis harnesses
│   └── sponjit_shadow/          shadow simulator; not the current bank path
│
├── SPONJIT_ARCHITECTURE.md      current SpongeJIT architecture overview
├── SPONJIT_FOUNDRY_SSA.md       current foundry/SSA atom vocabulary design
├── SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md online fusion planner specification
├── SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md   native stencil ABI specification
└── SPONJIT_RUNTIME_DESIGN.md    future runtime/materializer specification
```

## Current status

### Interpreter VM (`src/`)

The main VM is a Lalin experiment for implementing Lua runtime structures and bytecode execution as typed regions.

It includes:

- runtime data products/layouts
- value, table, stack, call, closure, upvalue, string, GC, error, parser, and API regions
- opcode dispatch/handler generation
- parser/compiler experiment pieces
- unit/integration tests under `tests/`

It does **not** currently call into SpongeJIT or install native images at runtime.

### SpongeJIT (`spongejit/`)

SpongeJIT is an adjacent prototype for an offline-trained native-stencil catalog.

Current implementation shape:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ structural loop-region records for paired loop bytecode (`src/loop_regions.lua`)
→ semantic SSA (`src/ssa_lift.lua`, `src/ssa_ir.lua`, `src/ssa_opt.lua`)
→ hole-parametric Stencil IR (`src/ssa_to_stencil.lua`, `src/stencil_ir.lua`)
→ canonical Stencil IR hash/form (`src/stencil_normalize.lua`)
→ global normal-form equivalence classes (`src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua`)
→ abstract native-stencil descriptors for unique supported forms (`src/stencil_lower.lua`)
→ ABI-lowered stencil metadata (`src/stencil_desc.lua`, `include/sponbank.h`)
```

The PUC Lua 5.5 C-function materializer and benchmarks were removed during the native-stencil ABI hard-yank. `spongejit/puc/` now documents that no maintained executable PUC integration exists until a native stencil linker is implemented.

## SpongeJIT build notes

From `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/`:

```sh
make test
make stencils        # build chunked SQLite foundry metadata
make bank             # build abstract libsponbank.so from stencil_bank.sqlite
```

`build_stencils.sh` is the maintained chunked/parallel metadata build. It generates weighted grammar chunks, runs `src/worker_compile.lua` in parallel into per-worker SQLite DBs, then runs `src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua` to produce the global normal-form DB. The dedupe key is the SSA-consumed canonical normal form (`canonical_stencil_hash`), not the source opcode sequence or fact contract; source patterns are retained as aliases:

```text
build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite
```

`build_bank.sh` consumes that SQLite DB and emits an abstract `libsponbank.so` exposing the `sponbank.h` descriptor/selector API. The bank is metadata-only: entries are flagged `SPON_STENCIL_ABSTRACT`, `spon_stencil_data()` returns no native bytes, and runtime materialization must still defer until native bodies exist. There is intentionally no maintained C-function tile build or PUC runtime build in this foundation.

The metadata bank requires LuaRocks modules on the LuaJIT path:

```sh
luarocks --local install lua-cjson
luarocks --local --lua-version=5.1 install lsqlite3
```

Default metadata-bank build knobs:

```sh
CHUNKS=128
WORKERS=$(nproc)
MAX_ARITY=4
MAX_FACT_COMBOS=32
FACT_AXIS_MODE=curated
WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS=1000
SPON_TMP=experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/build/stencil_bank
```

Do not confuse these offline stencil metadata experiments with the Lalin interpreter VM in `src/`. Building SpongeJIT artifacts does not automatically make `src/vm_loop.lua` JIT-enabled.

## Non-goals / warnings

- This directory is not a production Lua implementation.
- The Lalin interpreter VM and SpongeJIT are not one integrated JIT system today.
- `tools/sponjit_shadow/` is an older shadow harness; do not treat it as the current stencil pipeline.
- Generated stencil metadata under `spongejit/build/` is configuration-dependent and can be removed with `make clean`.

# SponJIT Architecture — Native Stencil Vocabulary + Online Fusion

**Status:** current design overview. The maintained implementation currently produces abstract native-stencil metadata. No executable SponJIT runtime is wired into the Lalin VM.

---

## One-sentence architecture

SponJIT builds a vocabulary of preverified native-stencil atoms offline, then Tier 2 performs long-range online fusion over those atoms to produce a `FusionPlan` that a mechanical copy/link/patch materializer can turn into one executable image.

```text
foundry/offline:
  bytecode/facts -> SSA -> Stencil IR -> abstract/native stencil atoms

Tier 2 online:
  hot atom span + facts -> FusionPlan

materializer/future:
  FusionPlan + NativeStencilBank -> LinkedImage
```

---

## Current implementation reality

The live maintained pipeline is metadata-only:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ abstract native-stencil descriptor for one representative
→ ABI-lowered stencil metadata
```

Key modules:

```text
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua
spongejit/src/ssa.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_projection.lua
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua
```

Current chunked outputs from `spongejit/build_stencils.sh` / `make stencils`:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

`make bank` consumes `stencil_bank.sqlite` and builds an abstract `build/sponbank/libsponbank.so` descriptor/selector library. It does not contain executable native bytes; it is the C ABI view of the current atom vocabulary.

There is currently no maintained executable SponJIT runtime, no native stencil linker, and no Lalin VM integration.

---

## Vocabulary

### Atom

An **atom** is a precomputed stencil template in the bank vocabulary. It may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. The layer does not matter to Tier 2 once the atom is in the vocabulary.

### Stencil

A **native stencil** is a relocatable native-code region with explicit endpoints, data relocations, control relocations, fact transfer, clobbers, and projection metadata.

An **abstract stencil** is the current metadata-only descriptor. It has no executable bytes and cannot be materialized.

### FusionPlan

A `FusionPlan` is the Tier 2 product: a long-range composition of atom instances plus explicit success edges, exit edges, fact flow, projection obligations, seam decisions, and materialization choices.

### LinkedImage

A `LinkedImage` is the future runtime product: one executable image created by copying stencil bytes, patching data/control relocations, installing projection stubs, and publishing it atomically.

---

## Phase tree

| Phase | Product | Current? |
|---|---|---|
| Foundry enumeration | opcode/fact atom candidates | yes |
| Semantic SSA | fact-specialized semantic graph | yes |
| Stencil IR | canonical hole-parametric shape | yes |
| Abstract stencil lowering | metadata-only stencil descriptor | yes |
| Tier 2 online fusion planner | `FusionPlan` over atoms | design / next work |
| Native stencil emission | executable stencil bytes + real relocs | future |
| Copy/link/patch materializer | `LinkedImage` | future |
| Lalin VM integration | active image execution from `src/vm_loop.lua` | gated off |

---

## Foundry role

The foundry creates the atom vocabulary. It may do expensive work offline:

- enumerate local atom tuples;
- apply fact combinations;
- build semantic SSA;
- lower to Stencil IR;
- validate stencil descriptors;
- dedupe canonical normal forms after SSA consumes semantics;
- rank useful variants;
- retain selected atoms for later composition.

The working saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 useful fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is not a Tier 2 trace limit. It is a vocabulary target.

---

## Tier 2 role

Tier 2 does long-range fusion over atom streams.

```text
4000 atoms
  -> online fusion planner
  -> one FusionPlan
  -> one LinkedImage after materialization
```

Tier 2 may optimize by choosing among prebuilt atoms, variants, endpoints, bridges, and projection stubs:

- direct atom-to-atom continuations;
- hot fallthrough layout;
- bridge insertion or omission;
- store/load seam removal;
- box/unbox seam removal;
- redundant guard removal;
- projection-stub sharing;
- cold exit outlining;
- dependency-aware variant choice.

Tier 2 must not synthesize arbitrary native instructions. It optimizes the stencil graph, not machine code.

---

## Why granular atoms are enough

Small atoms give the fusion planner flexibility. If the bank has atom variants with precise contracts, a long fused image can be trace-like without a runtime code generator.

The required contracts are:

- input/output locations;
- clobbers;
- fact transfer;
- data relocations;
- control relocations;
- projection recipes;
- dependency obligations;
- endpoint compatibility.

If atoms only expose frame-synced boundaries, Tier 2 mainly removes dispatch/linking overhead. If atoms expose register/value-flow endpoints and reconstructing projections, Tier 2 can remove frame seams and behave much more like a trace optimizer.

---

## Runtime role

The future runtime is deliberately simple compared with a tracing compiler:

```text
observe stable hot span
  -> invoke online fusion planner over bank atoms
  -> materialize chosen FusionPlan
  -> publish active image
  -> on exit, project state and fall back/demote/replan
```

The runtime does not run SSA, invent new instructions, infer projection recipes, or patch live images in place.

---

## Floor and active image

When executable SponJIT exists, each enabled region has:

```text
floor   always-correct native baseline image
active  current selected image; may equal floor
```

The floor requires total native coverage for the enabled opcode subset. Until such a floor exists, SponJIT remains a foundry/planner prototype and the Lalin VM uses its normal interpreter path.

---

## Relation to Lalin VM

The Lalin interpreter VM in `src/` is not JIT-integrated today. `src/contract.lua` keeps:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

SponJIT must not become an execution engine for `src/vm_loop.lua` until VM gates around bytecode validation, frame/cache semantics, native ABI, allocation, errors, and coroutine/yield behavior are satisfied.

---

## Canonical documents

| Document | Role |
|---|---|
| `SPONJIT_FOUNDRY_SSA.md` | Foundry/SSA atom vocabulary design. |
| `SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` | Online fusion planner data/control spec. |
| `SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md` | Native stencil ABI and copy-link-patch conformance. |
| `SPONJIT_RUNTIME_DESIGN.md` | Future runtime/materializer design. |
| `README.md` | Directory status and test commands. |

---

## Non-goals

SponJIT does not currently:

- execute native stencil images;
- integrate with the Lalin VM;
- ship an executable stencil bank;
- maintain a runtime code cache;
- run a LuaJIT-style IR optimizer/emitter at runtime;
- rely on C-function-shaped stencils as a conforming backend.

---

## One-line rule

```text
The foundry creates atoms.
Tier 2 chooses and proves a long fusion over atoms.
The materializer copies, links, patches, and publishes exactly that proof.
```

# SponJIT Foundry SSA — Atom Vocabulary Design

**Status:** current design/implementation notes for the maintained metadata pipeline. The foundry currently emits abstract native-stencil metadata, not executable code.

---

## One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT foundry enumerates local atom candidates, specializes them with explicit facts through semantic SSA, lowers useful candidates to Stencil IR and abstract stencil descriptors, and builds the vocabulary that Tier 2 later fuses over long hot spans.

---

## Current pipeline

The maintained pipeline is:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ lower one representative per supported class to an abstract native-stencil descriptor
→ ABI-lowered metadata
```

Equivalence class key for grammar/foundry mode:

```text
canonical_stencil_hash
```

The source opcode sequence and fact-transfer contract are **not** part of the normal-form key. Many opcode/fact candidates may collapse to one final form after SSA. Their source patterns are retained as aliases for selection; the stencil body/descriptor is emitted once.

## Loop boundary stance

`FORPREP`/`FORLOOP` and `TFOR*` are not scalar opcodes for the current fact-axis pass. They are structural loop-region boundaries. Until SpongeJIT has paired loop-region lowering with explicit loop edges, full loop-window slotmaps, topology bindings, and edge-sensitive fact transfer, the foundry must not advertise i64 loop fact bundles. Numeric and generic loops therefore remain explicit residual region boundaries, not partially specialized stencils.

Important modules:

```text
spongejit/src/grammar_enum.lua       -- grammar / sequence enumeration
spongejit/src/enumerate.lua          -- corpus/window enumeration
spongejit/src/loop_regions.lua       -- paired numeric/generic loop topology records
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua           -- opcode/facts -> semantic SSA
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua             -- SSA graph/value/effect model
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua            -- local semantic cleanup
spongejit/src/ssa.lua                -- public compile facade
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua          -- reopen serialized node specs
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua         -- canonical hole-parametric shape
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua     -- SSA -> Stencil IR
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua  -- canonical key/hash/form
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua       -- Stencil IR -> abstract stencil
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua        -- stencil schema, validation, ABI lowering
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua     -- worker SQLite metadata artifacts
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua   -- global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering
```

Chunked metadata-bank build:

```sh
cd experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit
make stencils        # CHUNKS=128, WORKERS=$(nproc) by default
make bank             # metadata-only libsponbank.so from stencil_bank.sqlite
```

`CHUNKS` and `WORKERS` are separate controls. More chunks reduce per-worker shard size; more workers increase parallelism.

SQLite outputs:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

Bank outputs:

```text
build/sponbank/libsponbank.so
build/sponbank/sponbank_build_manifest.json
```

The generated shared library is an abstract metadata bank, not executable native text. It exists so selectors and descriptor consumers can use the current `sponbank.h` API while the native body emitter/linker remains future work.

---

## Foundry vs Tier 2

The foundry builds local vocabulary. Tier 2 builds long-range fusions.

```text
Foundry:
  generate useful atoms and variants

Tier 2 online fusion planner:
  compose many atoms into one FusionPlan
```

A local atom may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. Tier 2 does not care which layer produced it; it sees a stream of atom candidates with explicit contracts.

---

## Atom vocabulary target

The current saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 useful fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is enough material to test the Tier 2 thesis because long traces are not precomputed as single giant atoms. Tier 2 composes many local atoms:

```text
4000 atom hot span
  -> FusionPlan over 4000 atom instances
  -> one future LinkedImage
```

The arity/fact-combo cap shapes the vocabulary. It does not cap the Tier 2 optimization range.

---

## Layer growth model

The foundry basis grows by selecting useful atoms from local compositions:

```text
A0 = raw opcode atoms
for layer k:
  candidates = local tuples over A_k, usually arity 2..4
  for candidate in candidates:
    for fact_env in selected fact combinations:
      ssa = specialize_with_facts(candidate, fact_env)
      stencil = lower_to_stencil(ssa)
      stencil = lower_to_abstract_stencil(stencil)
      validate / score / dedupe
  A_{k+1} = A_k ∪ selected_atoms
```

Only selected atoms enter the vocabulary. Rejected or unselected candidates are measurements, not runtime artifacts.

---

## Semantic SSA role

Semantic SSA is the fact-consuming layer.

It is responsible for:

- preserving opcode semantics;
- making fact consequences explicit;
- modeling effects, memory tokens, guards, exits, dependencies, and value residencies;
- exposing source PC and exit metadata;
- producing a graph suitable for Stencil IR lowering.

It is not a runtime IR. The future runtime must not build or optimize SSA.

---

## Stencil IR role

Stencil IR is the canonical materialization shape between semantic SSA and stencil descriptors.

It represents:

- stencil ops;
- values;
- data holes;
- slotmaps;
- facts;
- exits;
- source ops;
- effect/projection-relevant metadata.

Important invariant:

```text
exit/fail are not data holes
```

Exits are endpoint/projection/control concepts.

---

## Stencil lowering role

`stencil_to_stencil.lua` lowers Stencil IR into abstract stencil descriptors.

Current first surface:

```text
i64 slot loads/stores
i64 guards
unbox/box-relevant i64 operations
simple constants/immediates
simple residual/boundary/unlowered exits
```

Supported simple data roles:

```text
slot
slot_store
imm
const
bool
```

Payload-heavy roles require explicit payload leases and dependency invalidation before executable use.

---

## Output atom contract

A selected atom must eventually expose enough structure for Tier 2 fusion:

```text
AtomTemplate:
  semantic identity
  source span / layer identity
  fact transfer
  endpoint contracts
  input/output locations
  clobbers
  data relocations
  control relocations
  projection recipes
  dependency obligations
  cost / score metadata
  abstract or native body phase
```

If endpoint and projection contracts are too weak, Tier 2 cannot do meaningful seam optimization even with many atom variants.

---

## Fact combinations

The working target is roughly 32 useful fact combinations per atom shape, after curation and ranking.

Facts are not just one bitmask in the design model:

```text
FactState = signature bits + payload leases + dependency obligations
```

The current simple surface is mostly signature-bit based. Shape, field, call-target, barrier, and dependency-heavy facts must not be smuggled through dummy values.

---

## Foundry scoring

The foundry should rank atoms by evidence and usefulness:

- frequency in corpus/profile data;
- estimated dispatch removal;
- estimated seam-removal opportunity;
- guard/projection cost;
- code size;
- dependency complexity;
- endpoint compatibility usefulness;
- contribution to later-layer basis growth.

The shipped vocabulary should be deduped and coverage-driven, not a raw Cartesian explosion.

---

## Relationship to Tier 2 online fusion

Tier 2 needs atom variants that expose choices:

```text
frame-synced endpoint variant
register-preserving endpoint variant
guardful variant
guardless variant when facts prove it
store/load variant
no-store/no-load seam variant
bridge atom
projection stub
```

The foundry does not precompute a whole 4000-atom trace. It precomputes the atom vocabulary that lets the online fusion planner construct such a trace.

---

## Non-goals

The foundry does not currently:

- emit executable native bytes;
- generate a runnable bank;
- provide a native linker/materializer;
- integrate with the Lalin VM;
- require the runtime to build SSA;
- rely on C-function-shaped stencils as a conforming backend;
- patch unsupported payload/dependency roles with dummy values.

---

## Current checks

```sh
luajit experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_real_ssa.lua
luajit experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_ir.lua
luajit experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_projection.lua
luajit experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_to_stencil.lua
luajit experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_retirement.lua
(cd experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit && make test)
```

---

## One-line rule

```text
The foundry saturates local atom vocabulary.
Tier 2 extracts long-range trace behavior by composing those atoms.
```

# SponJIT Runtime Design — Native Stencil Image Materialization

**Type:** future runtime/materializer specification  
**Methodology:** explicit programming  
**Status:** design; not implemented in the maintained tree  
**Depends on:** `SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` and `SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md`

---

## 0. Current reality

The maintained SponJIT implementation currently stops at **abstract native-stencil metadata**:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ abstract native-stencil descriptor for one representative
→ grammar_worker_N.sqlite → stencil_bank.sqlite
```

There is currently no maintained executable SponJIT runtime:

- no C-function tile bank;
- no maintained PUC runtime integration;
- no native stencil linker;
- no executable L0 floor image;
- no copy/link/patch materializer;
- no Lalin VM integration.

The Lalin interpreter VM gate remains closed:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

This document is therefore not a description of working code. It is the runtime design that becomes valid only after the online fusion planner has a proof-bearing stencil vocabulary and a native stencil bank exists.

---

## 1. One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT runtime observes hot spans, invokes an online fusion planner over preverified native stencil templates, materializes the chosen fusion plan by copying bytes and patching data/control relocations, publishes that image atomically, and falls back through projection-safe exits without running arbitrary SSA optimization, register allocation, or code generation at runtime.

That sentence is the boundary:

```text
runtime may:       observe, plan fusion over bank stencils, select, copy, link, patch, publish, demote, log
runtime may not:   compile arbitrary code, synthesize instructions, infer hidden semantics, execute abstract stencils
```

---

## 2. Relationship to the other SponJIT specs

### 2.1 Online fusion planner spec

`SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` defines the planner used in two binding-time modes.

Current design/proof mode:

```text
abstract stencils + facts + projections
→ FusionPlan / RejectionReport / Deferral
```

Future runtime mode:

```text
native stencil bank + observed facts + site memory
→ FusionPlan / FloorOnly / RejectionReport / Deferral
```

The runtime does not run a general compiler. It may run the online fusion planner over prebuilt native stencil templates. That planner can choose variants, bridges, endpoints, seams, and layout policies from the bank vocabulary; the materializer then realizes the chosen plan mechanically.

### 2.2 Native stencil ABI spec

`SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md` defines the future native stencil ABI:

```text
native bytes + endpoint metadata + data relocs + control relocs + projection recipes
```

This runtime design is the consumer of that ABI.

### 2.3 Runtime design boundary

This document starts at the first point where the system has a conforming native stencil bank:

```text
NativeStencilBank
  -> online fusion planning
  -> FusionPlan
  -> MaterializationPlan
  -> LinkedImage
  -> active execution
```

If the only available artifacts are abstract stencils, runtime materialization must return:

```text
Deferred(NeedsNativeStencils)
```

---

## 3. Retired terminology and replacement terminology

The old documents used `TileTemplate`, C stencils, and C-function-shaped tiles. That terminology is retired for maintained SponJIT runtime design.

Use these terms instead:

| Old term | New term | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| TileTemplate | NativeStencilTemplate | Relocatable native code region with explicit endpoints. |
| tile image | LinkedImage | Executable region image built from stencil instances. |
| tile exit | StencilExit | Non-success endpoint reached from native stencil code. |
| tile-site memory | StencilSiteMemory | Per-span selector memory for exits, penalties, cooldowns. |
| C stencil | non-conforming artifact | C functions are not linkable native stencils. |

content.text:
### Stencil

A **stencil** is a relocatable native code region with explicit entry and exit
endpoints.

A stencil is not a C function.

### Endpoint

An **endpoint** is a named control position inside a stencil:

- `ENTRY`
- `OK`
- `GUARD_FAIL`
- `RESIDUAL`
- `BOUNDARY`
- `RETURN`
- `BRANCH_TRUE`
- `BRANCH_FALSE`

### Data relocation

A **data relocation** patches a value consumed by native code: slot offset,
immediate, constant, helper address, dependency address, payload address, etc.

### Control relocation

A **control relocation** patches a control-transfer target: success continuation,
guard failure target, residual target, branch target, projection stub, return target.

### Projection

A **projection** reconstructs interpreter-visible state at a non-success exit.

### Physical ABI

A **physical ABI** defines architecture, platform calling convention, fixed register
roles, clobber rules, stack rules, and context/frame locations.

---

## 4. Non-goals and prohibitions

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT treat existing `exit`/`fail` metadata
holes as native branch targets unless they are retyped as control relocations.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT delete a frame store across an exit unless
that exit has a complete projection recipe.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT patch unsupported hole roles with dummy
values.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT infer clobbers or endpoint locations by
reverse-engineering machine code.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT require runtime instruction encoding for
ordinary stencil linking.

---

## 5. Descriptor model

A stencil catalog MUST provide these descriptor classes:

```text
SponPhysicalAbiDesc
SponStencilDesc
SponEndpointDesc
SponLocationDesc
SponDataRelocDesc
SponControlRelocDesc
SponFactTransferDesc
SponEffectDesc
SponProjectionDesc
SponProjectionEntryDesc
SponDependencyDesc
```

The binary representation is implementation-defined, but the semantic fields in
this specification are mandatory unless explicitly marked optional.

---

## 6. Physical ABI descriptor

### 6.1 Required fields

```text
SponPhysicalAbiDesc:
  abi_id:              u16

[740 more lines in file. Use offset=180 to continue.]

```text
SponStencilDesc:
  stencil_id:          u32
  physical_abi_id:      u16

  text_offset:          u64
  text_size:            u32
  entry_offset:         u32

  source_start_pc:      u32
  source_len:           u16
  semantic_class:       u32

  endpoint_start:       u32
  endpoint_count:       u16

  input_start:          u32
  input_count:          u16
  output_start:         u32
  output_count:         u16
  clobber_start:        u32
  clobber_count:        u16

  data_reloc_start:     u32
  data_reloc_count:     u16
  ctrl_reloc_start:     u32
  ctrl_reloc_count:     u16

  fact_transfer_id:     u32
  effect_id:            u32

  projection_start:     u32
  projection_count:     u16

  dependency_start:     u32
  dependency_count:     u16

  flags:                u32
```

### 8.2 Rules

`entry_offset` MUST be within `[0, text_size)`.

Every endpoint offset MUST be within `[0, text_size)` unless the endpoint is
represented purely by a control relocation site.

A stencil MUST declare at least one `ENTRY` endpoint.

A stencil that can continue on the hot path MUST declare one `OK` endpoint or one
success control relocation.

A stencil that can fail a guard MUST declare a `GUARD_FAIL` endpoint or guard-fail
control relocation.

A stencil that may return to the VM MUST declare `RETURN` or `BOUNDARY` semantics.

A stencil MUST declare all data and control relocations required for materialization.

A stencil MUST NOT require implicit fallthrough unless the descriptor marks the
`OK` endpoint as layout-adjacent fallthrough-capable.

---

## 9. Endpoint descriptor

```text
SponEndpointDesc:
  stencil_id:      u32
  kind:             EndpointKind
  offset:           u32
  input_start:      u32
  input_count:      u16
  output_start:     u32
  output_count:     u16
  fact_require_id:  u32
  fact_produce_id:  u32
  projection_id:    u32    // zero if not applicable
  flags:            u32
```

Endpoint kinds:

```text
ENDPOINT_ENTRY
ENDPOINT_OK
ENDPOINT_GUARD_FAIL
ENDPOINT_RESIDUAL
ENDPOINT_BOUNDARY
ENDPOINT_RETURN
ENDPOINT_BRANCH_TRUE
ENDPOINT_BRANCH_FALSE
```

Rules:

An `ENTRY` endpoint MUST define required input locations.

An `OK` endpoint MUST define produced output locations and fact transfer.

Non-success endpoints that can resume outside the fused image MUST reference a valid
projection recipe or explicitly declare `SYNCED_FRAME` projection.

---

## 10. Relocation descriptors

## 10.1 Data relocation kinds

```text
DATA_RELOC_SLOT_OFFSET
DATA_RELOC_SLOT_STORE_OFFSET
DATA_RELOC_IMM32
DATA_RELOC_IMM64
DATA_RELOC_CONST
DATA_RELOC_BOOL
DATA_RELOC_SHAPE_ID
DATA_RELOC_FIELD_OFFSET
DATA_RELOC_ARRAY_BASE_OFFSET
DATA_RELOC_CALL_TARGET
DATA_RELOC_BARRIER_CELL
DATA_RELOC_DEPENDENCY_CELL
DATA_RELOC_HELPER_ADDR
```

### 10.2 Control relocation kinds

```text
CTRL_RELOC_OK
CTRL_RELOC_GUARD_FAIL
CTRL_RELOC_RESIDUAL
CTRL_RELOC_BOUNDARY
CTRL_RELOC_RETURN
CTRL_RELOC_BRANCH_TRUE
CTRL_RELOC_BRANCH_FALSE
CTRL_RELOC_PROJECTION_STUB
CTRL_RELOC_FALLBACK_STUB
```

### 10.3 Data relocation descriptor

```text
SponDataRelocDesc:
  stencil_id:      u32
  code_offset:      u32
  reloc_encoding:   RelocEncoding
  kind:             DataRelocKind
  op_idx:           u16
  arg:              i32
  width:            u8
  flags:            u32
```

### 10.4 Control relocation descriptor

```text
SponControlRelocDesc:
  stencil_id:      u32
  code_offset:      u32
  reloc_encoding:   RelocEncoding
  kind:             ControlRelocKind
  source_endpoint:  EndpointKind
  target_policy:    TargetPolicy
  op_idx:           u16
  flags:            u32
```

### 10.5 Relocation encodings

At minimum, an implementation SHOULD support:

```text
RELOC_ABS64
RELOC_REL32
RELOC_IMM32
RELOC_IMM64
```

Rules:

A relocation write MUST fit the declared width and encoding.

A control relocation target MUST be resolved after final layout.

A data relocation target MUST be resolved before executable permission is installed.

Unsupported relocation kinds MUST reject the stencil image.

---


[455 more lines in file. Use offset=465 to continue.]

```text
SponStencilImage:
  pc_start:             u32
  pc_end:               u32
  physical_abi_id:      u16
  entry_addr:           pointer
  text_addr:            pointer
  text_size:            u32
  instance_count:       u16
  projection_count:     u16
  observed_sig:         u64
  entry_sig:            u64
  flags:                u32
```

Rules:

An image MUST contain one executable allocation for the hot linked body and its cold
stubs, unless platform constraints require a documented split.

An image MUST NOT be published until all relocations are patched and executable
memory permissions are installed.

On platforms requiring instruction-cache maintenance, the runtime MUST perform it
before publication.

---

## 16. Fusion planner and linker algorithm

The online fusion planner is the proof-producing optimization phase. It chooses the stencil instances, variants, bridges, success edges, projection recipes, and seam-removal strategy from the bank vocabulary.

The linker/materializer is the proof-consuming mechanical phase. It MUST NOT invent new machine code, infer projection recipes, delete stores/guards, or perform semantic optimization not present in the `FusionPlan`.

The combined Tier 2 pipeline MUST perform these phases in order or prove an equivalent ordering:

```text
1. Build candidate stencil set from the bank.
2. Online fusion planner selects/proves a FusionPlan.
3. Verify physical ABI compatibility.
4. Resolve canonical slots to actual frame slots.
5. Validate required facts and payload dependencies.
6. Validate endpoint input/output compatibility.
7. Insert only bridge stencils selected by the FusionPlan.
8. Validate clobbers against live endpoint/projection values.
9. Validate projection completeness for all non-success exits.
10. Assign final layout offsets.
11. Resolve control targets from explicit plan edges.
12. Allocate writable image memory.
13. Copy stencil text.
14. Apply data relocations.
15. Apply control relocations.
16. Install projection/fallback stubs.
17. Change memory permissions to executable.
18. Publish image atomically.
```

At any failed validation or materialization step, the linker MUST reject the candidate image and leave the currently active valid image unchanged.

---

## 17. Endpoint compatibility

An edge from producer endpoint `P` to consumer endpoint `C` is compatible iff:

```text
P.output_count satisfies C.input_count
for each required C input:
  there exists a live P output or prior live value with same ValueType and Location
required facts of C are available after P fact transfer
P clobbers do not destroy values needed by C or by exit projections
```

If not compatible, the linker MAY insert bridge stencils.

A bridge stencil MUST itself satisfy this specification.

If no legal bridge sequence is chosen by the fusion plan, the image MUST be rejected.

---

[205 more lines in file. Use offset=715 to continue.]

SSA / Stencil IR              -> semantic source for stencil descriptors
AbstractStencilTemplate      -> metadata-only; not executable
SponStencilDesc-shaped ABI   -> lowered metadata view; provisional wire shape
DataReloc / ControlReloc      -> separated relocation classes
Endpoint / Projection metadata-> required for future native materialization
```

There is no maintained executable stencil bank or runtime linker today. Any old callable-function tile ABI is retired and non-conforming. The target architecture is native stencils plus a proof-consuming Copy-Link-Patch materializer, not a dual ABI.

---

## 21. Initial conformance surface

The first conforming stencil set SHOULD be limited to:

```text
i64 slot loads
i64 slot stores
i64 guards
i64 add/sub/mul-like arithmetic
immediates/constants
simple success continuations
guard failure exits with synced-frame or register projection
```

The first conforming stencil set SHOULD NOT include:

```text
shape payloads
field payloads
array payloads
call target payloads
barrier/dependency cells
may_call/may_gc/may_yield stencils
```

This is not a reduced ABI. It is the first conformance surface for validating the
full ABI rules without unresolved payload machinery.

---

## 22. Required validation experiments

### 22.1 Metadata sufficiency

For each candidate stencil class, verify that descriptors can name:

```text
entry endpoint
success endpoint
failure endpoints
inputs
outputs
clobbers
data relocations
control relocations
fact transfer
effects
projection recipes
dependencies
```

### 22.2 Linkability

Given representative i64 stencil sequences, verify:

```text
endpoint matches
bridge insertions
spill insertions
control target resolution
fallthrough eligibility
rejection reasons
```

### 22.3 Projection correctness

For every non-success endpoint, verify:

```text

[77 more lines in file. Use offset=843 to continue.]

For every executable linked image, compare against:

```text
PUC interpreter behavior
floor/native-stencil image behavior
expected frame state
expected exit state
expected resume pc/op index
```

---

## 23. Conformance checklist

A stencil catalog conforms to this specification iff:

```text
[ ] every stencil has a valid physical ABI descriptor
[ ] every stencil has explicit entry metadata
[ ] every control transfer target is represented by endpoint/control-reloc metadata
[ ] every data patch site is represented by data-reloc metadata
[ ] every stencil declares inputs, outputs, and clobbers
[ ] every stencil declares fact transfer
[ ] every stencil declares effects
[ ] every non-success exit has a projection descriptor
[ ] every dependency is explicit and runtime-validatable
[ ] unsupported holes/dependencies reject image construction
[ ] no C-function return boundary is required for hot success flow
```

An online fusion planner conforms iff:

```text
[ ] it chooses only stencils, variants, and bridges present in the bank
[ ] it validates facts and dependencies before selecting a stencil instance
[ ] it validates endpoint compatibility before creating a success edge
[ ] it proves any store/load, box/unbox, or redundant-guard seam it removes
[ ] it assigns every non-success endpoint a complete projection obligation
[ ] it emits explicit plan edges; no semantic edge is implicit fallthrough
[ ] it records typed rejection/deferral reasons
```

A runtime linker/materializer conforms iff:

```text
[ ] it consumes a FusionPlan rather than inventing semantic optimizations
[ ] it validates physical ABI compatibility
[ ] it resolves canonical slots
[ ] it validates clobbers against live values/projections
[ ] it validates projection completeness remains true after layout
[ ] it patches data relocations
[ ] it patches control relocations from explicit plan edges
[ ] it publishes only fully patched executable images
[ ] it leaves the current valid image active on rejection
```

---

## 24. One-line rule

```text
A function stencil is a sealed callable tile.
A native stencil stencil is a linkable region with explicit continuations.
A Tier 2 fusion plan is the proof that those continuations compose.
```

Copy-Link-Patch requires native stencil stencils and a proof-consuming materializer.

summary:
No prior history.

---

**Turn Context (split turn):**

## Original Request
Find everything relevant to turning current SpongeJIT abstract stencil descriptors into executable native byte stencils. Focus areas: descriptor schema, `build_bank` emission, runtime ABI, tests, old object/ELF helpers, and exact places currently forcing `executable=false`, `size=0`, `data=NULL`. Report concrete facts and risks, not proposals.

## Early Progress
- Enumerated `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit`: key files include `include/sponbank.h`, `src/stencil_desc.lua`, `src/stencil_lower.lua`, `src/build_bank.lua`, `src/worker_compile.lua`, `src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua`, `src/elf_parser.lua`, `build_stencils.sh`, `build_bank.sh`, tests, and empty `runtime/`.
- Found current implementation is explicitly metadata-only:
  - Docs/README say foundry emits abstract native-stencil metadata, not executable native text.
  - `build_bank.lua` comment says it emits an abstract non-executable `libsponbank.so`.
  - `bench/README.md` says no maintained executable runtime/native materializer exists.
  - `puc/README.md` says no PUC executable integration until native linker consumes current stencil descriptors.
- Inspected `stencil_lower.lua`:
  - Comment says current descriptors are abstract and non-executable.
  - Data relocs are emitted with `code_offset = 0`, `code_offset_kind = "abstract_zero"`.
  - Control relocs are also emitted with `code_offset = 0`, `code_offset_kind = "abstract_zero"`.
- Inspected `sponbank.h`:
  - Defines `SponExecCtx`, stencil flags `SPON_STENCIL_ABSTRACT = 1`, `SPON_STENCIL_NATIVE = 2`, `SPON_STENCIL_PUC_PATCHABLE = 4`.
  - Defines ABI/location/endpoint/data-reloc/control-reloc enums and accessor API:
    `spon_get_stencil`, `spon_stencil_data`, `spon_stencil_endpoints`, `spon_stencil_data_relocs`, `spon_stencil_control_relocs`, etc.
- Inspected build pipeline:
  - `build_stencils.sh`: grammar enumeration → SSA workers → per-worker SQLite → global dedupe → lower unique supported forms once.
  - `build_bank.sh`: consumes `build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite`, generates C shards via `src/build_bank.lua`, compiles with GCC, links `libsponbank.so`.
  - `worker_compile.lua` does not lower descriptors; `dedupe_normal_forms.lua` performs unique lowering.
- Inspected tests:
  - `test_spongejit_stencil_desc.lua` validates abstract descriptor schema and ABI numeric lowering.
  - `test_spongejit_stencil_lower.lua` asserts all data/control relocs use `abstract_zero`.
  - `test_spongejit_retirement.lua` forbids old tile/fragment ABI names.
- Inspected generated bank artifacts:
  - Generated `bank_chunk_*_stencils.inc` entries show stencil offset/size fields as `0, 0` and flags `1` (`SPON_STENCIL_ABSTRACT`).
  - Generated `bank_index.c` implements `spon_stencil_data(SponStencilId id)` as `(void)id; return NULL;`.
  - Existing generated manifest reports `61831` stencils, `597491` candidates, `393396` patterns, 2 shards.
- Queried current SQLite bank:
  - `225560` unique forms total.
  - `61831` lowered OK, `163729` not lowered.
  - All lowered stencil JSONs have `executable=false`.
  - All lowered layouts: `mode="abstract_stencil"`, `executable=false`, `code_offsets="abstract_zero"`.
  - All ABI stencils have `(offset,size)=(0,0)` and flags `1`.
  - All reloc offsets are `(0,"abstract_zero")`.
- Inspected old object/ELF helpers:
  - `spongejit/src/elf_parser.lua` and `src/jit/elf_parser.lua` are minimal binutils-based parsers using `nm -S --defined-only` and `objdump -d -r`.
  - `tools/jit_harness/object_mine.lua` mines object bytes/holes/relocs/clobbers but currently treats body range as whole object payload `{offset=0,size=object_size}`.
  - `candidate_compile.lua` can compile C kernels to `.o`; `candidate_emit.lua` emits old `jit_stencil_v0` kernels, not current `sponbank.h` ABI.
  - `tools/sponjit_shadow/stencils` is archived; README says it is not current contract. It contains old `stencils.c`, `stencil_abi.h`, `extract_stencils.lua`, and `stencil_library.json` with byte/holes metadata.
- Inspected old executable experiments:
  - Benchmarks contain prior `objdump .text`, `mmap` RWX, FFI/function-pointer experiments.
  - These are precedent/risk only, not maintained SpongeJIT runtime.

## Context for Suffix
- The key “forced abstract” locations already found are:
  - `stencil_lower.lua`: emits `code_offset=0`, `code_offset_kind="abstract_zero"` for data/control relocs.
  - `build_bank.lua` / generated shard data: emits abstract descriptors with offset/size `0,0`, flags `SPON_STENCIL_ABSTRACT`.
  - Generated `bank_index.c`: `spon_stencil_data()` always returns `NULL`.
  - SQLite stencil JSON: `executable=false`, layout reason “metadata-only stencil descriptor; no native assembler offsets emitted”.
- Runtime/native execution is absent:
  - `spongejit/runtime/` is empty.
  - No maintained materializer, executable memory manager, or PUC integration exists.
- Current ABI metadata exists and is numeric-lowered, but native byte payloads and concrete relocation offsets do not.
- Old ELF/object tooling exists but belongs to retired/archived or harness paths and does not directly match current `SponStencilDesc` / `sponbank.h` ABI.
